added_assumptions.label: ass:inference-regularity
added_assumptions.statement: Inference regularity for the 'inference' conjecture (latent, restricted to the observed cells and the fixed lambda/regime used in the proof). (i) Primitive i.i.d. finite-cell sampling: O_i=(X_i,M_i,Y_i) i.i.d. with overlap, i.e. eta(P)=(rho_{xm},mu_{xm}) in the relative interior of the four-cell probability simplex so r_{x,m}=mu_{xm}/rho_{xm} are well defined. (ii) Finite locally-C^1 active-branch representation of the endpoint maps L,U: the sharp endpoints admit the finite piecewise-C^1 vertex maps v^L_j, v^U_j of Open-ended Question 4 (oeq:vertex), with well-defined active indices on interior regimes and a finite kink set. This is the modular dependency of the inference result (OEQ 5) on the endpoint geometry (OEQ 4) plus standard sampling; it does NOT alter the identification theorems (collapse, interval, non-decomposition).
added_assumptions.source: meta-orchestrator grant (latent regularity; faithful to gate-accepted spec's regular/directional inference rung; headline ID results unaffected)

added_assumptions.label: A-mediator-shift
added_assumptions.statement: Active mediator channel (front-door non-degeneracy): the treatment shifts the mediator law, i.e. q_{·|1} ≠ q_{·|0} (equivalently ∃ m with q_{m|1} ≠ q_{m|0}). All strict-inclusion / non-decomposition claims (Conjecture non-decomposition parts (b)-(c)) are made on the sub-regime {P : κ(P) ≠ 0 and q_{·|1} ≠ q_{·|0}}; the ⊆ inclusion of part (a) is unconditional.
added_assumptions.source: Standard front-door non-degeneracy: when q_{·|1}=q_{·|0} the M→Y mediator channel carries no treatment variation, so the shared-U structure cannot tighten beyond the box/sensitivity bounds — it is the regime kernel the conjecture must condition on, not the headline ID result, and it leaves the collapse/interval/vertex/inference results untouched.

flags.rewound_from_stage0: Case 2(2c) regime_defining theorem-split (over Case 6b stage_neg1, which fails because the proposal is salvageable: part (a), conj:vertex, conj:inference all reproduce; only conj:nondecomp (b)-(c) is false). Regression pattern (a): same nondecomp counterexample flagged in both reviews (revise then reject) — a stable, localized conjecture-level defect with a named fix, not wandering. The {κ(P)≠0} regime is the wrong characterization (counterexample has κ=1/2 yet no strict inclusion when q_{·|1}=q_{·|0}); the true regime additionally needs an active mediator channel q_{·|1}≠q_{·|0}. Over-precision test (2a') fails (this restricts WHICH laws, not an attainment/closure form), so split rather than statement_correction. loop_guard clear (theorem_splits=0); split is not collapsed (conditional strict inclusion still requires the shared-U LP/compatibility argument).
flags.stage0_stitch_invariant_violated: dangling reference \ref{eq:vlp}: no matching \label{eq:vlp} in the document. Define the referenced object (e.g. emit the missing \begin{...}\label{eq:vlp}...\end{...} environment) or remove the citation.

proposed_from.topic: FIELD-tier partial identification of the LEAKY FRONT-DOOR model. Binary X (treatment), binary M (mediator), bounded outcome Y in [0,1], latent confounder U with finite support (Caratheodory-reduced to <= |X||M|+1). DAG edges U->X, U->Y, X->M, M->Y, plus the LEAK X->Y; the mediator is CLEAN (U independent of M given X). Outcome structural mean phi(m,x,u)=E[Y|M=m,X=x,U=u] in [0,1]; latent controlled-direct-effect d(m,u)=phi(m,1,u)-phi(m,0,u); a SINGLE scalar leak bound |d(m,u)|<=lambda, with lambda=0 = Pearl exclusion. Observed law p_x, q_{m|x}=P(M=m|X=x), r_{x,m}=E[Y|X=x,M=m]. Because M is clean, P(U|X,M)=P(U|X), giving FOUR outcome-compatibility equations r_{x,m}=sum_u phi(m,x,u) P(U=u|X=x). do(X=x) cuts U->X, so ACE = sum_m sum_u [q_{m|1} phi(m,1,u) - q_{m|0} phi(m,0,u)] P(U=u). The identified set I(lambda)={ACE : there exist (P(U), P(U|X), phi in [0,1]) meeting the four compatibility equations, the X-marginal, and |d|<=lambda} is a function of the observed law and lambda alone, a singleton at lambda=0 (Pearl front-door point identification), so the partial identification arises PURELY from the bounded confounded direct path (no sensitivity on a non-identified parameter). TARGET THEOREMS: (1) NON-DECOMPOSITION: because the SAME P(U) and P(U|X) appear in all four compatibility equations and in ACE, I(lambda) is STRICTLY inside both the independent per-mediator controlled-direct-effect box (whenever P(U|X=1) differs from P(U|X=0)) and the generic latent-confounding sensitivity bounds (which lack the clean-mediator compatibility equations); (2) CLOSED-FORM finite-vertex sharp endpoints with an explicit kink regime where the r_{x,m} constraints switch from slack to binding; (3) a cross-fit/DML estimator of the endpoints with regular root-n inference over the interval, directional at the kink. Differentiate from Pearl front-door (lambda=0), Ding-VanderWeele 2016 mediation sensitivity (mediator-outcome confounding, a different violation), and generic proxy / information-theoretic confounding bounds (which ignore the clean mediator).
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue material is eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator, which records non-rectangular shared-latent front-door geometry but does not define the single-clean-mediator leaky direct-path ACE set I(lambda), and pid_proximal_l2_bridge, which concerns proximal bridge endpoint geometry and nonsmooth inference in a different proxy model. This proposal's kernel is the shared-U leaky front-door interval, with strict non-decomposition now scoped to kappa(P) != 0 and q_.|1 != q_.|0.","published_axis":"Pearl1995CausalDiagrams supplies only the lambda=0 front-door point; FulcherShpitserMarealleTchetgen2017GeneralizedFrontDoor changes the target to a population intervention indirect effect; DingVanderWeele2016SharpMediationSensitivity studies mediator-outcome confounding rather than a clean mediator plus direct structural leak; BalkePearl1997Bounds and FinkelsteinAdamsSariaShpitser2020MeasurementError provide finite latent LP templates but not the bilinear shared-U leaky front-door endpoint construction or its inference."}
seed_list: We characterize the sharp ACE identified set I(lambda) for the leaky front-door model (clean mediator, single scalar direct-leak bound lambda) and prove it collapses to Pearl's front-door functional at lambda=0, conjecture strict inclusion inside the per-mediator CDE box and the generic latent-confounding sensitivity interval whenever Pr(U|X=1)!=Pr(U|X=0), give closed-form finite-vertex endpoints with a kink threshold lambda*, and pose a cross-fitted directional-inference estimator.

Prove the non-rectangularity theorem: I(lambda) is strictly inside the outer product of per-mediator controlled-direct-effect intervals because one shared latent law P(U|X) couples all four clean-mediator compatibility equations, strict exactly when the confounder shifts across arms.

Derive a closed-form finite-vertex (Caratheodory K<=5) formula for the sharp endpoints L(lambda),U(lambda) of the leaky front-door interval, with an explicit threshold lambda* where the outcome-compatibility constraints switch from slack to binding.
Construct a cross-fitted/DML estimator of the leaky front-door endpoints with root-n Gaussian inference in the interior of an active regime and a directional limit at the kink lambda*.

Give a comparison theorem with necessary-and-sufficient conditions for when proxy/proximal partial-ID intervals coincide with, strictly contain, or are incomparable to the clean-mediator leaky front-door interval I(lambda).
Prove that I(0) equals Pearl's front-door functional as a singleton, certifying the leaky model is a strict generalization with an exclusion-recovering corner.

Establish the monotone sensitivity-scale structure: I(lambda) is compact with L nonincreasing and U nondecreasing and nested in lambda, and compute the critical lambda at which 0 enters I(lambda) (sign-ambiguity breakdown).

Prove that the leaky front-door interval and the Ding-VanderWeele mediator-outcome-confounding sensitivity set are generically incomparable because they encode different structural violations of the same DAG.
Prove the Caratheodory sufficiency: every endpoint of I(lambda) is attained by a latent law with support at most |X||M|+1=5, reducing the bilinear program to a finite-support search.

Characterize how the interval narrows when the single scalar leak bound lambda is replaced by a heterogeneous per-(m,u) radius lambda(m,u), and identify which components of the radius are point-identified from the compatibility equations.

seed_details.one_line: We characterize the sharp ACE identified set I(lambda) for the leaky front-door model (clean mediator, single scalar direct-leak bound lambda) and prove it collapses to Pearl's front-door functional at lambda=0, conjecture strict inclusion inside the per-mediator CDE box and the generic latent-confounding sensitivity interval whenever Pr(U|X=1)!=Pr(U|X=0), give closed-form finite-vertex endpoints with a kink threshold lambda*, and pose a cross-fitted directional-inference estimator.

anchor_paper.author: Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: The front-door functional point-identifies the ACE of X on Y when the direct X->Y path is excluded and the mediator is clean.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift sharpening anchored to Pearl front-door: STRONGER -- the shared latent law makes the bilinear set non-rectangular (not a mechanical lambda-band on Pearl's point), so the relaxation acquires a new coupling absent at lambda=0; flagged that it could be phrased as M4 LP-default but the program is genuinely bilinear (M-non-LP).
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Ding-VanderWeele 2016 mediation sensitivity bounds because it is just a bounded-direct-effect band around the front-door estimate.
seed_details.rebuttal: Ding-VanderWeele parameterize mediator-outcome confounding with a dirty mediator and excluded direct path; here the mediator is clean and the four compatibility equations plus one shared P(U|X) couple the latent law to the effect, so I(lambda) is generally NOT a band around theta_FD and is strictly inside the rectangular box (Conjecture 3).

seed_details.one_line: Prove the non-rectangularity theorem: I(lambda) is strictly inside the outer product of per-mediator controlled-direct-effect intervals because one shared latent law P(U|X) couples all four clean-mediator compatibility equations, strict exactly when the confounder shifts across arms.

anchor_paper.author: eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator (prior proposal)
anchor_paper.claim: Shared latent coordinates can make an outer product of marginal intervals nonsharp; reviewers requested a rational small-instance witness.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: must not confuse the outer LP with a sharp identified set

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism-decomposition sharpening: STRONGER -- pins the nonsharpness to the shared P(U|X) clause and gives the regime (w1!=w0) where it bites; orthogonal to a generic Manski LP.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from any partial-ID LP because added constraints shrink the set.
seed_details.rebuttal: Inclusion is trivial; the content is the STRICTNESS characterization tied to w1!=w0 and a finite rational witness off the box segment, which a rectangular relaxation cannot certify.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a closed-form finite-vertex (Caratheodory K<=5) formula for the sharp endpoints L(lambda),U(lambda) of the leaky front-door interval, with an explicit threshold lambda* where the outcome-compatibility constraints switch from slack to binding.

anchor_paper.author: Balke and Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Finite response-type enumeration plus LP yields sharp closed-form causal bounds in a nonparametric discrete model.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends LP enumeration to a bilinear shared-U program: STRONGER -- the leak constraint and shared latent law make the program bilinear, so standard LP duality does not apply and the vertex set/kink must be derived anew.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Balke-Pearl LP since discrete causal bounds are always finite-vertex.
seed_details.rebuttal: Balke-Pearl is a linear program; the clean-mediator compatibility equations are bilinear in (phi,w), so finite-vertex sharpness and the slack-to-binding threshold lambda* are not a corollary of LP geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a cross-fitted/DML estimator of the leaky front-door endpoints with root-n Gaussian inference in the interior of an active regime and a directional limit at the kink lambda*.

anchor_paper.author: Fang and Santos
anchor_paper.claim: The ordinary bootstrap is inconsistent for directionally differentiable functionals unless the functional is fully differentiable.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimation-identification combo (M8): STRONGER -- the min/max endpoint map is nonsmooth with an active-set kink, so pointwise delta and ordinary bootstrap fail; directional inference is the genuine content.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Fang-Santos plus Chernozhukov-Hong-Tamer because inference for set-identified kinked functionals is solved.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those give general templates; the contribution is the specific cross-fitted plug-in into the leaky front-door vertex map, the directional derivative along the binding compatibility face, and the regime-complete case split at lambda=0/interior/lambda*.

seed_details.one_line: Give a comparison theorem with necessary-and-sufficient conditions for when proxy/proximal partial-ID intervals coincide with, strictly contain, or are incomparable to the clean-mediator leaky front-door interval I(lambda).

anchor_paper.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Proxy variables partially identify causal effects without completeness, producing nonsmooth observed-law bound endpoints.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge/comparison sharpening: WEAKER as standalone -- the two settings have different latent-information structures, so a clean iff is hard; kept as supplementary (sec11), not a numbered result.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because the two intervals are about different latent structures and need not relate.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partial rebuttal only; precisely because they need not nest, an iff characterization is nontrivial -- but the angle is thin enough that it is demoted to a supplementary direction, not the headline.

seed_details.one_line: Prove that I(0) equals Pearl's front-door functional as a singleton, certifying the leaky model is a strict generalization with an exclusion-recovering corner.

anchor_paper.author: Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: The front-door functional point-identifies the ACE under exclusion.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target/regime corner check: this is a sanity Theorem, not a novel kernel; n/a -- orthogonal.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Pearl 1995, it is just the lambda=0 substitution.
seed_details.rebuttal: Acknowledged routine; it functions as the required reduction (Theorem 1 / Corollary 1), not as the contribution.

seed_details.one_line: Establish the monotone sensitivity-scale structure: I(lambda) is compact with L nonincreasing and U nondecreasing and nested in lambda, and compute the critical lambda at which 0 enters I(lambda) (sign-ambiguity breakdown).

anchor_paper.author: Ding and VanderWeele
anchor_paper.claim: A scalar sensitivity radius produces monotone sharp mediation bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity critical-delta (M3): STRONGER iff the critical lambda (0 enters) is closed-form; WEAKER if only qualitative monotonicity. Folded into Theorem 2 + Conjecture 4.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because enlarging a constraint radius enlarges the feasible set.
seed_details.rebuttal: Monotonicity is routine; the closed-form critical lambda for sign ambiguity is the content and is subsumed by the vertex/kink conjecture.

seed_details.one_line: Prove that the leaky front-door interval and the Ding-VanderWeele mediator-outcome-confounding sensitivity set are generically incomparable because they encode different structural violations of the same DAG.

anchor_paper.author: Ding and VanderWeele
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp bounds under unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding, a violation distinct from a bounded clean-mediator direct leak.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Dispute/foil sharpening: WEAKER as standalone -- incomparability is expected; better used to position Conjecture 3 than as its own result.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because the two models violate different assumptions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Weak; demoted to a positioning remark in sec5 rather than a numbered theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Prove the Caratheodory sufficiency: every endpoint of I(lambda) is attained by a latent law with support at most |X||M|+1=5, reducing the bilinear program to a finite-support search.

anchor_paper.author: Finkelstein, Adams, Saria, and Shpitser
anchor_paper.claim: Discrete latent partial-ID problems reduce to finite linear-constraint systems.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/finite-support sharpening: enabling lemma rather than headline; n/a -- orthogonal (supports Conjecture 4).
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Caratheodory's theorem for moment problems.
seed_details.rebuttal: The dimension count |X||M|+1 must be derived from the number of binding compatibility equations; routine but load-bearing, so used as Assumption 1's justification, not a headline.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize how the interval narrows when the single scalar leak bound lambda is replaced by a heterogeneous per-(m,u) radius lambda(m,u), and identify which components of the radius are point-identified from the compatibility equations.

anchor_paper.author: Fulcher, Shpitser, Marealle, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: A generalized front-door admits a direct exposure-outcome path, motivating heterogeneous direct-effect structure.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Heterogeneity sharpening: STRONGER iff a component of lambda(m,u) is point-identified; WEAKER if routine reparametrization. Left as a follow-up axis.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the scalar-lambda result by replacing lambda with lambda(m,u).
seed_details.rebuttal: Partial; the nontrivial part is which radius components the four equations pin down, but the scalar case is the cleaner field-tier headline, so this is deferred.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Pearl (1995, Biometrika, Pearl1995CausalDiagrams): front-door criterion point-identifies ACE under exclusion of X->Y; this is the lambda=0 corner of our set.
Fulcher-Shpitser-Marealle-Tchetgen (2017, arXiv, FulcherShpitserMarealleTchetgen2017GeneralizedFrontDoor): generalized front-door allows a direct path but re-targets to a population intervention indirect effect, not a sharp total-effect leak interval.
Ding-VanderWeele (2016, Biometrika, DingVanderWeele2016SharpMediationSensitivity): sharp mediation sensitivity bounds for unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding -- a DIFFERENT violation (dirty mediator, excluded direct path); used as foil.
Balke-Pearl (1997, JASA, BalkePearl1997Bounds): finite response-type LP template for sharp discrete causal bounds; method ancestor for finite-vertex endpoints.
Finkelstein-Adams-Saria-Shpitser (2020, arXiv, FinkelsteinAdamsSariaShpitser2020MeasurementError): discrete latent + conditional-independence assumptions encoded as linear constraints for sharp bounds.
Ghassami-Yang-Shpitser-Tchetgen (2021, arXiv, GhassamiYangShpitserTchetgen2021HiddenMediators) and Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen (2023, arXiv, GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023ProxyPartialID): proxy/proximal partial-ID for hidden mediators/confounders without completeness -- distinct latent-information structure; comparison is supplementary.
Chernozhukov-Hong-Tamer (2007, Econometrica, ChernozhukovHongTamer2007ParameterSets): criterion-based confidence regions for set-identified parameters; the inference framework for OEQ 5.
Fang-Santos (2019, ReStud, FangSantos2019DirectionallyDifferentiable): ordinary bootstrap fails for directionally differentiable functionals -- needed at the kink lambda*.
Manski (1990, AER, Manski1990NonparametricBounds): bounded-outcome partial-ID antecedent for the [0,1] box on phi.
Live tension: no located paper gives sharp finite-vertex partial ID plus regular/directional inference for a front-door model whose only violation is a scalar-bounded confounded direct path with a clean mediator.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 / headline interval language
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Connectedness of I(lambda) is not proved; the settled conditional claim is sharp endpoint bounds [L(lambda),U(lambda)] unless OEQ4 also proves the full attainable set is an interval.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Stage 0 should derive compactness/nesting/endpoints, not silently upgrade to full-set interval connectedness.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 4
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Finite active-branch and kink formula are open construction targets, scoped to the K<=5 finite latent-support convention and the stated no-kink convention.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Endpoint formula is conditional on constructing the active-cell rational representation rather than assumed as already available.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 5
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Inference is conditional on the OEQ4 finite locally-C1 active-branch representation plus Assumption ass:inference-regularity.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Stage 0 should treat inference as a dependent construction question, not an independent proved estimator theorem.

banked_reason: Proposed field kernel (non-decomposition: I(lambda) strictly inside the per-mediator CDE box / generic confounding bounds) is mathematically FALSE — the latent confounder saturates the box even under the active-mediator regime q_{.|1}!=q_{.|0} (pipeline salvage: nondecomp Type C unsalvageable; confirmed by decomposition ACE=theta_FD - p1*sum(q_{m|1}-q_{m|0})e_m + sum q_{m|1}f_m where extra latent atoms decouple e_m,f_m to the box corners). Sound reusable subfield content: I(lambda)=B_box(lambda) is the SHARP identified interval with closed-form response-type-LP vertex endpoints + kink-robust (Fang-Santos/CHT) inference — a front-door leak-sensitivity tool. Possible negative-result reframe: a clean front-door mediator gives NO robustness dividend against a bounded confounded direct path (the naive box is already sharp).
